frameworks.name: reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: dopamine firing tracks updates in predicted reward over time

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making on random-dot motion
frameworks.one_line: extends RPE coding to perceptually demanding discrimination stimuli

core_question: Do midbrain dopamine neurons extract reward predictions from perceptually complicated stimuli, and how does that response unfold in time?
key_assumptions: dopamine activity reflects reward prediction and prediction error
coherent motion direction is the reward-predictive feature requiring sensory processing
coarse motion detection precedes fine direction discrimination
manipulations: two coherent motion directions paired with different reward magnitudes
random-dot motion stimuli requiring directional discrimination
key_findings: dopamine neurons showed reward-predictive responses to random-dot motion
responses were temporally extended, tracking prediction updates from coarse detection to fine discrimination
differential reward-predictive activity emerged in a later phase than previously reported
interpretations: dopamine prediction signals are paced by the sensory evidence accumulation required to identify the cue
reward prediction error operates across coarse-to-fine perceptual scales rather than as a single transient
